mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS crosses above 5.0%, tighter policy raises the discount rate applied to equity cash flows and reduces risk appetite, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or the mean triggered return is not below the unconditional mean at p <= 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A positive 3-month trend in RSAFS signals resilient household demand and stronger expected revenues, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the RSAFS 3-month trend has Rank IC below 0.03 with 20-day forward returns over any 3-year rolling window or positive-trend months do not outperform negative-trend months.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE crosses upward by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months, labor-market deterioration lowers expected earnings growth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE crosses upward by > 0.3pp over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations have positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of cases or if average triggered returns are not lower than non-triggered returns at p <= 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: High FEDFUNDS combined with a falling 3-month trend in HOUST signals rate-sensitive housing stress that spills into credit and consumption, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint FEDFUNDS-HOUST trigger does not deliver lower 60-day returns than either single condition alone, or if interaction coefficient is not negative at p <= 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A negative 3-month trend in INDPRO signals weakening production and inventory-cycle pressure, reducing expected operating leverage and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if negative INDPRO 3-month changes are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if return differences versus positive INDPRO changes are statistically indistinguishable.

mechanism_narrative: Rising CPIAUCSL while FEDFUNDS is at or above 5.0% signals inflation pressure despite restrictive policy, forcing tighter financial conditions and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint CPIAUCSL-FEDFUNDS condition has non-negative average 20-day excess returns or if the interaction term is not negative in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Positive PAYEMS momentum with UNRATE not rising confirms labor-income support without recessionary unemployment stress, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-UNRATE trigger produces 5-day returns below the unconditional mean in more than 50% of rolling 2-year windows or if the interaction coefficient is non-positive.

mechanism_narrative: A positive quarterly trend in GDPC1 signals broad real-demand expansion and stronger aggregate earnings capacity, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive GDPC1 quarterly growth fails to generate higher 60-day S&P 500 returns than non-positive growth in walk-forward tests or if Rank IC is below 0.03 for two consecutive 3-year windows.
